meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Overcome No Design Skills
content.seo_description: Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit to overcome design hurdles. An intermediate checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first project.
content.answer_block: Overcome design limitations by leveraging a pre-built SaaS starter kit. By integrating Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you bypass the UI bottleneck. Focus on business logic and functionality while your tech stack handles the aesthetic layer, turning your prototypes into professional-grade production software.
content.intro:
You have the vision, the logic, and the drive, but your screen remains a graveyard of unstyled divs and broken layouts. The 'no-design' curse is the silent killer of student projects, turning promising SaaS ideas into abandoned GitHub repositories. It is time to stop pretending you are a UI/UX expert and start building like a senior engineer.

By adopting a high-performance SaaS starter kit, you decouple your product's value from your ability to pick color palettes. We will utilize Antigravity and Claude Code to automate the heavy lifting, allowing you to ship a polished, functional platform while everyone else is still debating CSS frameworks.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Initialize your project structure using a battle-tested SaaS starter kit to ensure authentication, database, and billing are pre-configured.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a full-stack SaaS application starter template with Supabase, Next.js, and Tailwind CSS, focusing on a mobile-responsive dashboard layout.

steps.title: Define the Domain Model
steps.description: Use Claude Code to map out your database schema based on your project requirements, ensuring type safety from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Prisma schema for a project management tool including users, projects, tasks, and team relations with appropriate indexing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate prisma schema based on my requirements.md'

steps.title: Automate UI Component Generation
steps.description: Stop writing CSS from scratch. Use Antigravity to generate clean, accessible components that match your brand identity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable UI component library using Radix UI and Tailwind, specifically focusing on data tables, input forms, and modal dialogs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create dashboard components using shadcn/ui and tailwind'

steps.title: Implement Intelligent Logic
steps.description: Inject business logic into your frontend using Claude Code to handle API routes and server-side operations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a server-side action to handle user subscriptions using Stripe, ensuring proper error handling and database state updates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement stripe checkout flow in app/api/subscribe'

steps.title: Stress Test and Refine
steps.description: Use automated testing loops to identify edge cases in your UI and logic before deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Playwright tests for the primary user flow: signup, dashboard access, and project creation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'write e2e tests for user authentication flow'

pro_tips: Prioritize 'Design-as-Code' by using component libraries like Shadcn/UI; never write raw CSS for standard elements.
Use Claude Code to explain complex legacy code blocks in your starter kit to understand the underlying architectural patterns.
Always set up your environment variables as a local .env file before running any generation scripts to avoid credential leaks.
Leverage Antigravity's 'Refactor' mode to clean up your codebase every time you add a new feature to prevent technical debt.
Build for the 'Mobile-First' viewport; responsive design is easier when you handle mobile constraints before desktop expansion.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, focus on setting up CI/CD pipelines to automate your deployment process and start gathering user feedback.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the blueprint, and the expertise to build something that matters. Stop letting design hurdles stand in the way of your engineering potential. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
